perturbation.original_text: A. In-kind Payment: HDS will provide Celebrity with a one (1) year complimentary supply (i.e., at least 365 caplets) of Clotamin product during the Term.
perturbation.changed_text: A. In-kind Payment: HDS will provide Celebrity with a complimentary supply of Clotamin product during the Term.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific quantity of 'at least 365 caplets', the agreement becomes ambiguous about the extent of the 'complimentary supply'. This creates an in-text contradiction with the intent of paragraph 5, which aimed to provide a concrete benefit to Celebrity. Without the explicit number, the value and enforceability of this consideration are significantly weakened, possibly leading to disputes over what constitutes a 'complimentary supply'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Consideration. As consideration for Celebrity's services under this Agreement, HDS agrees as follows: A. In-kind Payment: HDS will provide Celebrity with a complimentary supply of Clotamin product during the Term. B. Cash Payment: (1) HDS will provide payment of Fifty Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($50,000) made payable to Celebrity's agent. CSE, as follows $10,000 Within ten (10) days of the parties execution of this Agreement $15,000 Within 60 days of the parties execution of this agreement $25,000* Ten (10) days prior to the Production Session [*Should Company decide not to conduct the Production Session, then the $25,000 allocated for said Production Session ("Production Fee") will not be owed to Celebrity and notice should be given to CSE as soon as reasonably known by Company if Company does not intend to conduct the Production Session. However, if Company schedules the Production Session, then said Production Fee is non-refundable regardless if the Production Session occurs. ] (a) CSE, shall invoice HDS for these fees and HDS shall pay such invoice within ten (10) business days following receipt of the CSE invoice. (b) Payments shall be made to CSE and delivered to 600 Galleria Parkway, Suite 1900, Atlanta, Georgia 30339. (2) If HDS desires to add an additional market or state to the Markets for the Commercial to air during the Term, then HOS will pay Celebrity Five Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($5,000) for said additional state/market, and the parties will amend the Agreement to add the additional state/market accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'at least 365 caplets' providing a clear, quantifiable consideration. The changed text removes this specific quantity, making the 'complimentary supply' ambiguous. This directly contradicts the intention of paragraph 5, which aims to provide a concrete benefit to Celebrity. Without the explicit number, the value and enforceability of this consideration are significantly weakened, possibly leading to disputes over what constitutes a 'complimentary supply', and therefore making it an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: A. HDS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to Celebrity in the event Celebrity fails to perform the duties set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Such termination shall relieve HDS of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: A. HDS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to Celebrity in the event Celebrity fails to perform the duties set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'Such termination shall relieve HDS of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement,' it creates an in-text contradiction because Paragraph 5 outlines the consideration HDS owes Celebrity. With this sentence removed, it's now unclear whether HDS is still obligated to provide consideration even after terminating the agreement due to Celebrity's breach. This creates uncertainty in the enforcement of termination rights and the financial obligations of HDS, creating an in-text contradiction with the definition of 'consideration' in section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Consideration. As consideration for Celebrity's services under this Agreement, HDS agrees as follows: A. In-kind Payment: HDS will provide Celebrity with a complimentary supply of Clotamin product during the Term. B. Cash Payment: (1) HDS will provide payment of Fifty Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($50,000) made payable to Celebrity's agent. CSE, as follows $10,000 Within ten (10) days of the parties execution of this Agreement $15,000 Within 60 days of the parties execution of this agreement $25,000* Ten (10) days prior to the Production Session [*Should Company decide not to conduct the Production Session, then the $25,000 allocated for said Production Session ("Production Fee") will not be owed to Celebrity and notice should be given to CSE as soon as reasonably known by Company if Company does not intend to conduct the Production Session. However, if Company schedules the Production Session, then said Production Fee is non-refundable regardless if the Production Session occurs. ] (a) CSE, shall invoice HDS for these fees and HDS shall pay such invoice within ten (10) business days following receipt of the CSE invoice. (b) Payments shall be made to CSE and delivered to 600 Galleria Parkway, Suite 1900, Atlanta, Georgia 30339. (2) If HDS desires to add an additional market or state to the Markets for the Commercial to air during the Term, then HOS will pay Celebrity Five Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($5,000) for said additional state/market, and the parties will amend the Agreement to add the additional state/market accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the sentence 'Such termination shall relieve HDS of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement' directly contradicts paragraph 5, which specifies the consideration HDS owes Celebrity. By removing the relieving clause, it becomes uncertain whether HDS is still obligated to provide the consideration outlined in paragraph 5, even after terminating the agreement due to Celebrity's breach. This creates a clear and direct conflict, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: HDS agrees to provide and maintain at its own expense, the following insurance coverages: A. Commercial General Liability coverage of product liability with limits no less than $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate. B. Umbrella / Excess Liability coverage inclusive of product liability with limits not less than $5,000,000 per occurrence and aggregate. C. Media Liability insurance with limits not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and aggregate. The Celebrity shall be named as an additional insured on coverages A, B and C. Celebrity is afforded waiver of subrogation on coverages A, B and C. All policies listed under A, B and C should have a thirty (30) day notice of cancellation provision or endorsement. HDS will provide Celebrity's agent, CSE, with a certificate of insurance within five (5) days of its request for same.
perturbation.changed_text: HDS agrees to provide and maintain at its own expense, the following insurance coverages: A. Commercial General Liability coverage of product liability with limits no less than $1,000,000 per occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate. B. Umbrella / Excess Liability coverage inclusive of product liability with limits not less than $5,000,000 per occurrence and aggregate. C. Media Liability insurance with limits not less than $2,000,000 per occurrence and aggregate. The Celebrity shall be named as an additional insured on coverages A, B and C. All policies listed under A, B and C should have a thirty (30) day notice of cancellation provision or endorsement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'HDS will provide Celebrity's agent, CSE, with a certificate of insurance within five (5) days of its request for same,' it creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty in the enforceability of the insurance requirements. While HDS is obligated to maintain certain insurance coverages and name Celebrity as an additional insured, there's no longer a mechanism for Celebrity to verify that HDS has actually complied with these obligations. This weakens the protection afforded to Celebrity under the indemnity clause (Section 13) and the representations and warranties of HDS (Section 12), as Celebrity has no way to confirm that these safeguards are in place. This creates a contradiction with the intent of Section 14, which is to protect celebrity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. Indemnity. HDS shall be solely responsible for all liability arising out of production, distribution and sale of its product. HDS hereby agrees to indemnify, defend and hold harmless Celebrity, his agents, representatives and employees (referred to collectively as "Celebrity Indemnities") from and against any and all claims, actions, causes or action, damages, injuries, expenses, liabilities (joint and several), penalties fines, attorneys fees, court costs, and any other expenses incurred by Celebrity Indemnities arising out of (1) breach by HDS of any of the terms, representations or warranties made by HDS in this Agreement; or (2) HDS product liability or trademark patent or other proprietary right infringement; or (3) errors, omissions, fraudulent or negligent acts by HDS, its employees, agents or subcontractors in connection with (i) any advertising featuring Celebrity; (ii) with the performance of HDS' duties and obligations under this Agreement; (iii) with the production, distribution, promotion, marketing and sales of products including related product packaging; and/or (iv) with the operation and management of its production and distribution facilities, however caused, HDS shall not be obligated to indemnify Celebrity with respect to damages which are the result of the active negligence or willful misconduct of Celebrity.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the sentence 'HDS will provide Celebrity's agent, CSE, with a certificate of insurance within five (5) days of its request for same' directly contradicts Section 13 regarding Indemnity. Without the certificate of insurance, Celebrity lacks a mechanism to verify HDS's compliance with insurance obligations. This undermines Celebrity's ability to enforce the indemnity clause, creating a significant inconsistency and uncertainty regarding the protections afforded to Celebrity under the agreement.
